events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection decision directly to the required JSON file. This is a document comparison task, so I’m treating the charging chart as controlling and the misread sight-glass bubbles as a failed diagnostic/charging verification.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",

  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",

    \"Charging diagnosis relied on misread sight-glass bubbles instead of confirming charge against the manufacturer charging chart.\",
    \"System charge status is not verified through the required tested/inspected step and may be overcharged or undercharged.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Reject the charging and diagnostics inspection result until charge is rechecked using the manufacturer charging chart and specified operating conditions.\",
    \"Record required pressures, temperatures, superheat or subcooling values, ambient conditions, and equipment tag data, then correct refrigerant charge as needed.\",
    \"Retest system operation and submit corrected commissioning or inspection documentation.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed condition indicates the sight glass was interpreted incorrectly during charging diagnostics. Manufacturer charging charts, not bubble appearance alone, control charge verification. Because the work has reached the tested/inspected stage without a valid chart-based charge confirmation, the installed condition is defective and non-compliant with the referenced manufacturer documentation.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"Manufacturer charging charts\",
    \"Equipment manufacturer installation and charging instructions\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json && cat /app/answer.json"
